title: O Week Lecture
body:
Social Entrepreneurship Practicum (Comm3030)

Lecture: 0 week

Lecturer in Charge: Dr Douglas Long

Email: douglas.long@unsw.edu.au

We are condensing a 10-week term into 5 intensive weeks, so there will be high pressure from the outset. It is vital that everyone is fully committed to what we are doing and that you are willing and able to work cooperatively in lectures, workshops, and out-of-class activities.

Our focus this term will be on “St John Ambulance” as a living case study

Course Objectives

COMM3030 is a practicum course designed to integrate theory with experiential practice and involve authentic engagement with partner organisations.

There are two key objectives: 1. To demonstrate the importance and value of social enterprises in addressing social barriers, and 2. To facilitate the development of skills for supporting effective social enterprise initiatives no matter the organisation for which one works.

Weekly Course Format

1 x 1 hour lecture weekly (on campus) Tuesday 10AM – 11-00 AM; before related workshop

1-2 essential readings weekly Deepen understanding of core concepts

1 x 3-hour workshop weekly (on campus) Activity based, philosophy to practice, design thinking

Client meetings throughout term Generally held during workshop time

Project briefs are worked on in groups so expect to meet outside of fixed class time.

Assessments Assessment Task Weighting Length Due Date Individual / Group Project Scope 30% 1000 words Friday 17 January Individual at 5.00pm (Week 2)

Project 30% 10 minutes + Wednesday 5 Group Presentation + Q&A February at Q&A 5.00pm (Week 5)

Participation / Peer 20% 1,000 words Peer Evaluation – Individual Evaluation Sunday 9 February at 5 pm (Week 5)

Personal and 20% 1000 words Friday 7 February Individual Professional at 5.00pm Reflection (Week 5) The complete 'Assessment Details' document i s available in Assessment Hub on Moodie

Course Schedule Week “0” General background information on podcast

Week 1 Introduction to Social Impact & Social Enterprise, Wicked Problems and Systems Thinking

Week 2 Client Briefings

Week 3 Design Thinking

Week 4 Client Feedback on initial presentations

Week 5 Change, Logic and Leadership

Facilitation Team Douglas Long

In 1970, while living in a country town, I became aware that there were no local services available to help those who were in need - neither local religious or civic groups seemed to see this as an area of concern. Together with the senior local Police Officer and the Mayor, I was instrumental in establishing a volunteer-driven organisation that provided services ranging from being a support person in Court appearances, through budgetary and related advice, to assistance in finding employment and for interactions with government agencies.

For more than 25 years I have been providing advice and support to 3 social enterprise organisations in Sydney and the NSW Central Coast and, in 2020, I became a nonexecutive director of a NFP startup providing mental health services in South Australia. I am a Fellow of the Higher Education Association.

I am passionate about social issues and treating every person with unconditional respect. A key application of unconditional respect relates to the effective provision of social enterprise services.

Client

St John Ambulance

Please become familiar with the information that is on Moodle

Thank You

I look forward to meeting you at 10-00 on Tuesday morning, January 7.
